retrieved_evidence.title: Deep Tech: Unlocking New Engines of Growth for Singapore | Singapore EDB
retrieved_evidence.snippet: Open innovation also plays a crucial role, allowing large corporates to source technologies from startups while offering real-world test environments for technology refinement. For instance, EDP Renewables recently collaborated with Singapore startup EtaVolt to pilot technology aimed at extending the lifespan of solar panels. One significant challenge for deep tech startups is securing sustained financing throughout the longer commercialisation journey. To address this, SEEDS Capital, the investment arm of Enterprise Singapore, has increased its co-investment cap for deep tech startups from S$8 million to S$12 million and expanded its investment mandate to early growth-stage startups. retrieved_evidence.snippet: . It simply isn’t not a researcher's job to explore commercial potential. An R&D venture studio addresses this quandary by gaining the trust and partnering with these researchers while doing all the heavy lifting of a commercial exploration. We empower our students and community to be entrepreneurs of all kinds, creating scalable and ethical innovations that have economic and social impacts in the GBA and beyond. To support our students and faculty in their entrepreneurial pursuits, we offer a wide range of programs and resources, including mentorship, funding opportunities, networking events, seminars, talks, and competitions. retrieved_evidence.title: The R&D Venture Studio Playbook
retrieved_evidence.snippet: . It simply isn’t not a researcher's job to explore commercial potential. Moreover, there is no evidence that an organization’s endemic researchers or staff are inherently good at turning inventions into products or businesses: it is a very different skill set. As a result, most researchers would like to see their inventions commercialized, but they don’t do anything about it. An R&D venture studio addresses this quandary by gaining the trust and partnering with these researchers while doing all the heavy lifting of a commercial exploration. The venture studio becomes a translational extension of the lab , allowing the PI to engage in research translation while focusing on what he or she is

NACIE advises on issues relating to innovation, technology commercialization, and entrepreneurship. retrieved_evidence.snippet: . It simply isn’t not a researcher's job to explore commercial potential. Moreover, there is no evidence that an organization’s endemic researchers or staff are inherently good at turning inventions into products or businesses: it is a very different skill set. As a result, most researchers would like to see their inventions commercialized, but they don’t do anything about it. An R&D venture studio addresses this quandary by gaining the trust and partnering with these researchers while doing all the heavy lifting of a commercial exploration. retrieved_evidence.title: The R&D Venture Studio Playbook
retrieved_evidence.snippet: . It simply isn’t not a researcher's job to explore commercial potential. Moreover, there is no evidence that an organization’s endemic researchers or staff are inherently good at turning inventions into products or businesses: it is a very different skill set. As a result, most researchers would like to see their inventions commercialized, but they don’t do anything about it. An R&D venture studio addresses this quandary by gaining the trust and partnering with these researchers while doing all the heavy lifting of a commercial exploration. The venture studio becomes a translational extension of the lab , allowing the PI to engage in research translation while focusing on what he or she is

web_evidence.title: Starting a Business in France: Entrepreneur Visa (Talent Passport) 2026 | Hiliv
web_evidence.snippet: Note for visitors If you are in France on a short-stay visa (Schengen or tourist) without a residence permit, it is generally not possible to change status from within France. You will need to return to your country of origin and submit a Talent Passport application at the local French consulate. Short-stay visa holders are therefore excluded from this route, as the entrepreneur visa requires a long-stay visa application from abroad. This flexibility allows people already in France (e.g. students or early-stage entrepreneurs) to switch to the long-stay Talent permit, provided they meet all the criteria. web_evidence.title: French Tech Visa Guide: Elegibility, Benefits, and How to Apply in 2026 - Joorney
web_evidence.snippet: France has spent the last decade building one of Europe’s most compelling startup ecosystems — and it has backed that ambition with an immigration pathway designed to match it. The French Tech Visa, introduced in 2017 as part of the Passport Talent residence permit (now called Talent Card), offers non-EU entrepreneurs, tech employees, and investors a streamlined route to live and work in France. But for international professionals navigating the move, the process raises immediate practical questions: Which category applies to my situation? What does “recognized innovative company” actually mean in practice? And how do I build a case that satisfies French immigration authorities while keeping

web_evidence.title: France Startup Visa: Guide to French Tech Visa for Non-EU Founders - Wise
web_evidence.snippet: What is the France Startup Visa? France has the Talent Passport Visa to attract international talent and encourage economic activity. This France Startup Visa program helps non-EU self-employed and employees to develop their businesses. The Talent Passport Visa is a long-term visa with a validity of four years. However, you must apply for a multi-year residence permit within the first two months of your arrival in France. The Talent Passport visa allows you to bring your family members, including your spouse and children, up to 18 years old. If approved, and upon arriving, you and your family must visit a local police station in France and request a multi-year residence permit.1 [...] Source

retrieved_evidence.title: The R&D Venture Studio Playbook
retrieved_evidence.snippet: Create a supporting structure for your VBs Beyond a salary and a desk, your venture studio should offer resources to VBs to amplify their work and keep them focused on what they do best. In particular, hire support staff or partner with other teams in your organization so that your VBs can easily get help with event planning, market sizing, legal advice, intellectual property reviews, marketing and design, and so forth. Another key way to support your VBs is to form an advisory board for each channel. web_evidence.title: Starting a Business in France: Entrepreneur Visa (Talent Passport) 2026 | Hiliv
web_evidence.snippet: Note for visitors If you are in France on a short-stay visa (Schengen or tourist) without a residence permit, it is generally not possible to change status from within France. You will need to return to your country of origin and submit a Talent Passport application at the local French consulate. Short-stay visa holders are therefore excluded from this route, as the entrepreneur visa requires a long-stay visa application from abroad. This flexibility allows people already in France (e.g. students or early-stage entrepreneurs) to switch to the long-stay Talent permit, provided they meet all the criteria. Conclusion: launching your startup in France [...] If the main holder’s permit expires, f

web_evidence.title: French Tech Visa Guide: Elegibility, Benefits, and How to Apply in 2026 - Joorney
web_evidence.snippet: France has spent the last decade building one of Europe’s most compelling startup ecosystems — and it has backed that ambition with an immigration pathway designed to match it. The French Tech Visa, introduced in 2017 as part of the Passport Talent residence permit (now called Talent Card), offers non-EU entrepreneurs, tech employees, and investors a streamlined route to live and work in France. But for international professionals navigating the move, the process raises immediate practical questions: Which category applies to my situation? What does “recognized innovative company” actually mean in practice? And how do I build a case that satisfies French immigration authorities while keeping

web_evidence.title: France Startup Visa: Guide to French Tech Visa for Non-EU Founders - Wise
web_evidence.snippet: What is the France Startup Visa? France has the Talent Passport Visa to attract international talent and encourage economic activity. This France Startup Visa program helps non-EU self-employed and employees to develop their businesses. The Talent Passport Visa is a long-term visa with a validity of four years. However, you must apply for a multi-year residence permit within the first two months of your arrival in France. The Talent Passport visa allows you to bring your family members, including your spouse and children, up to 18 years old. If approved, and upon arriving, you and your family must visit a local police station in France and request a multi-year residence permit.1 [...] Source

web_evidence.title: Architecture strategies for safe deployment practices - Microsoft Azure Well-Architected Framework | Microsoft Learn
web_evidence.snippet: Another common deployment model is a blue-green approach. In this model, two identical sets, or pools, of workload infrastructure are deployed. Both pools are able to handle a full production load. The first (blue) pool runs the current version of the deployment where all users land. The second (green) pool is updated with the new feature and internally tested. After internal testing, a subset of the production traffic is routed from the blue pool to the green pool. Like canary deployments, the rollout is progressive as you shift more of the traffic over to the green pool in successively larger rollout waves. After you finish the rollout, the update pool becomes the blue pool and the green p

web_evidence.title: 6 Deployment Strategies (and How to Choose the Best for You) | LaunchDarkly
web_evidence.snippet: 5. Blue/green deployments A blue/green deployment is one in which the existing application (blue environment) and the new deployment (green) are independently running in two identical production environments. Once testing is complete on the green environment, live traffic is redirected to it making it live for all users. Maintaining two environments simultaneously means that there is no downtime and it allows for seamless rollbacks should an incident occur. One of the downsides is the costs of maintaining duplicates sets of infrastructure for the deployments. In fact, in many cases some resources like databases or other shared services are not duplicated, but shared by both the blue and gree

Blue/green deployments A blue/green deployment is one in which the existing application (blue environment) and the new deployment (green) are independently running in two identical production environments. Once testing is complete on the green environment, live traffic is redirected to it making it live for all users. Maintaining two environments simultaneously means that there is no downtime and it allows for seamless rollbacks should an incident occur. One of the downsides is the costs of maintaining duplicates sets of infrastructure for the deployments.
